pages.raw_text:
Representatives of the City
league asked the Central Labor
union last night to withdraw union
objection to their playing at Ma
tatorium park. They argued J. p
Graves had refused to build new
park and that they had gone to
great expense in preparing for the
coming season. They also said
they paid the w. w. p. Co. nothing
for the use of the grounds or for
transportation for the teams to and
from the park. After stating theiL
position the representatives of the
league withdrew


Members of the grounds commit
tee of the Central Labor union
presented resolution, which were
adopted, asking the business
houses not to permit their teams to
play at the park this year, 1t was
not claimed the unions had right
to boycott the firms represented in
the league, and the action of the
central body will be forwarded to
them in the nature of request
that they offer no aid to the local
monopoly by playing team in the
league.
